title: Troubleshoot Floci Azure resources stuck in 'Creating' state during deployment
description: AKS, ACR, and Redis cache containers are UP and healthy, but stuck in 'Creating' state on the Floci API, causing Terraform to poll indefinitely. Terminal diagnostics are currently blocked due to a system-level permission approval timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify why Floci API does not transition resource status to 'Succeeded'
Resolve the provisioning state blocker so Terraform apply finishes successfully
Acquire diagnostic logs from floci-az container
notes: deploy.ps1 fixed (Docker socket mounted, silent certificate import with certutil fixed).
Root cause identified: Docker bridge network DNS resolution is disabled, preventing Floci from resolving sub-container hostnames.
Authorized solution: deploy.ps1 modified to create and use a custom user-defined network (floci-network).
New blocker encountered: Port collision (ports 6379 and 6443) during rerun because orphaned sub-containers from the first run remain active on the host.
Proposed solution: Run a Docker command to clean up all orphaned floci-az sub-containers.
Rerun progress: ACR successfully provisioned in 11 seconds. AKS and Redis containers are Up and initializing.
AKS status: Currently booting nested K3s control plane and deploying system workloads, while Floci polls port 6443.
Workflow update: Parent cancelled manual task-277. Deployment engineer instructed to request command execution approval from @manager and run the deploy script themselves.
TLS blocker: Port exposure race condition caused TLS certificate download to fail silently before Quarkus HTTP server was fully ready, leading to x509 verification error.
TLS resolution: deploy.ps1 modified to implement a retry loop (up to 15 attempts with 2s sleep) for certificate downloads. Awaiting @manager approval to run the test.
